title: Does an abelian group of type [G16,G9,7] admit a (1008,266,70)-difference set?
lean_gate_reason: Lean would only seal the repaired side lemma excluding full order-7 coset unions. That is archival polish, not a human-ready seal for the selected [G16,G9,7] row.
micro_paper_assessment: Fails the micro-paper lane after publication audit. The surviving verified content is only a small structural exclusion of full order-7 coset unions, not a near-paper theorem packet for the selected row.
family_affinity: strong fixed-row Lander residue with a direct C7 descent and an order-16 character obstruction
generalization_signal: moderate because the final order-2-character obstruction is reusable once a p-part descent lands on a 2-group quotient with nonsquare norm target
strongest_honest_claim: A hypothetical (1008,266,70) difference set in a group of type [G16,G9,7] cannot be a union of the order-7 cosets; the solver's attempted descent to a (144,38,10) quotient is invalid.
candidate_theorem_slice: A hypothetical (1008,266,70) difference set in a group of type [G16,G9,7] cannot be a union of the order-7 cosets, because a 0/7 fiber profile contradicts the quotient identity XX^(-1) = 196*1_Q + 490*Q.
paper_title_hint: No recommended standalone paper title; at most a section-level lemma excluding full order-7 coset unions in the (1008,266,70) [G16,G9,7] case.
